capabilities.detail: Schedules values through the hidden X2 display register when display-state proofs hold.
Uses R0 fractional/alias indirect effects only under explicit exact-machine proofs.
Considers FA..FF one-command dispatch only after layout and selector proofs.
Overlays code, address operands, and constants only after post-layout conflict checks.
Reuses the current X value across lowering sites instead of spilling a temporary register.
Dispatches directly on one-shot input values without allocating persistent state.
Synthesizes indirect-flow targets with a solved address formula after layout.
Merges dispatch cases whose bodies are identical to the default branch.
Packs several small non-negative hidden fields into decimal digits of one register.
Carries boolean state bits in signs of positive hidden counters.
Uses compiler-owned setup-time branch selectors for one-cell indirect flow.
Routes branches to an existing В/О in physical cell 00 through one-cell indirect flow.
Reuses post-layout stop tails and existing selectors after final address proofs.

candidates.reason: compiler-owned address preload selected for one-cell indirect branch/call

machineFeaturesUsed.detail: Optimizer selected register-held branch addresses for one-cell indirect flow.
Optimizer scheduled hidden X2 values across display-byte or temporary boundaries.

proofs.detail: V2 state domains and expression ranges were tracked during lowering.

emulatorFacts.detail: В/О with an empty return stack behaves as one-cell БП 01 in continuous execution.
*F aliases behave like the corresponding *0 commands, including R0 transformation; they do not preserve R0.
Indirect К БП R with R=FA..FF executes one command at 48..53, then continues at 01..06.
Direct БП FA consumes/overwrites the following operand byte, while indirect К БП R leaves 01..06 usable as tail code.
К БП 0 with R0 in fractional positive/small states jumps to 99 and leaves R0=-99999999.
К П->X 0 and К X->П 0 with 0<R0<1 access R3 and leave R0=-99999999.
ВП, '.', '/-/', and digit-entry X2 restoration boundaries are modeled as display-state boundaries.
With 1|-00 in Y, multiplying by X and then normalizing through В↑ yields a zero/nonzero threshold at |X|=1.
In ГРД angle mode, cos(100) is zero and acos(0) is 100; optimizer use must be guarded by expected_mode("grd").
Continuous-run behavior is the default profile; step-only divergences are explicit exact-machine facts.
Opcode 5F leaves internal X intact but mutates display/raw state; it is usable only as an explicit display-state trick.
